proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (worse PFS for ECOG 1 and 2 vs 0).
stage_iv = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv = 0.
has_brain_mets = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets = 0.
histology = squamous is associated with shorter pfs_months than histology = adenocarcinoma.
Current smoking_status is associated with shorter pfs_months than former or never smoking.

analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by ECOG: 0=4.33, 1=3.19, 2=2.15. ANOVA p<1e-300.
Mean PFS stage_iv=1 minus stage_iv=0 = -1.44 mo. p<1e-300.
Mean PFS difference = -0.92 mo. p<1e-300.
Mean PFS squamous (2.81) minus adenocarcinoma (3.67) = -0.86 mo. p<1e-300.
Mean PFS: current=2.99, former=3.61, never=3.68. ANOVA p=3.7e-187.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation).
sex_female = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than sex_female = 0.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: r = +0.76, p ~ 0. Older patients have substantially longer PFS in this dataset (counterintuitive vs typical NSCLC literature).
Female mean PFS minus male = -0.20 mo. p=7.7e-25.
r = -0.019, p = 3.6e-5. Direction consistent with worse PFS at higher LDH but small magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Lab markers crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl have detectable univariate associations with pfs_months.

analyses.code: pearsonr for each lab variable vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: All of crp (p=0.78), nlr (p=0.42), hemoglobin (p=0.75), alk-phos (p=0.14), AST (p=0.70), ALT (p=0.20), bilirubin (p=0.41), creatinine (p=0.22), BUN (p=0.51), sodium (p=0.48), potassium (p=0.33), calcium (p=0.29) showed |r|<0.01 with no significant linear relationship to PFS. These labs appear to be noise variables in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: egfr_mutation = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months (typical NSCLC favourable prognosis).
kras_g12c = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months (typical adverse prognosis).
alk_fusion = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months (typical favourable prognosis).
stk11_mutation = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
brca2_mutation = 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months.
tmb_high = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: EGFR+ minus EGFR- = +0.19 mo. p = 6.5e-11. Direction matches expected favourable prognosis but small magnitude.
KRAS G12C+ minus KRAS G12C- = +0.78 mo. p = 7.4e-94. Direction is OPPOSITE the conventional adverse-prognosis expectation.
ALK+ minus ALK- = -0.81 mo. p = 5.6e-66. Direction is OPPOSITE the conventional favourable-prognosis expectation.
STK11+ minus STK11- = +0.009 mo. p = 0.74. No detectable effect; hypothesis refuted.
BRCA2+ minus BRCA2- = -0.12 mo. p = 0.031.
TMB-high minus TMB-low = -0.19 mo. p = 1.0e-17. Direction OPPOSITE expected favourable IO-related prognosis.
r = +0.006, p = 0.16. No detectable linear association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_sotorasib = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_olaparib = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.
treatment_osimertinib = 1 is associated with longer pfs_months overall.

analyses.result_summary: Pembro minus no-pembro = -0.035 mo. p = 0.081. Hypothesis refuted: no positive main effect.
Sotorasib minus no-sotorasib = +0.32 mo. p = 5.6e-49. Supported (likely driven by KRAS G12C+ subgroup; see iter 6).
Olaparib minus no-olaparib = -0.025 mo. p = 0.26. Hypothesis refuted.
Osimertinib minus no-osimertinib = +0.004 mo. p = 0.87. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_sotorasib provides a larger PFS benefit in patients with kras_g12c = 1 than in those with kras_g12c = 0 (positive interaction between sotorasib and kras_g12c).
treatment_osimertinib provides a larger PFS benefit in patients with egfr_mutation = 1 than in those with egfr_mutation = 0.
treatment_olaparib provides a larger PFS benefit in patients with brca2_mutation = 1 than in those with brca2_mutation = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Sotorasib effect in KRAS+ = +2.55 mo; in KRAS- = -0.005 mo. Interaction coef = +2.56, p < 1e-300. Strongly supported.
Osimertinib effect in EGFR+ = -0.014 mo; in EGFR- = +0.006 mo. Interaction p = 0.76. Hypothesis refuted: osimertinib does NOT preferentially benefit EGFR-mutant patients in this cohort.
Olaparib effect in BRCA2+ = +0.010 mo; in BRCA2- = -0.026 mo. Interaction p = 0.78. Hypothesis refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab provides a larger PFS benefit in patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.5 than in those with pdl1_tps < 0.5.
treatment_pembrolizumab provides a larger PFS benefit in tmb_high = 1 patients than in tmb_high = 0 patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab effect varies with histology (squamous vs adenocarcinoma).
treatment_pembrolizumab effect varies with stk11_mutation (worse with STK11 co-mutation, established literature signal).

analyses.result_summary: Pembro effect in PD-L1 high = -0.07 mo; in PD-L1 low = -0.025 mo. Interaction p = 0.36. Refuted.
Pembro effect in TMB-high = -0.048; in TMB-low = -0.030. Interaction p = 0.68. Refuted.
Pembro effect in squamous = -0.074; in adenocarcinoma = -0.014. Interaction p = 0.17. Not significant.
Pembro effect in STK11+ = -0.049; in STK11- = -0.032. Interaction p = 0.77. Refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within KRAS G12C+ patients, the sotorasib benefit on pfs_months is larger in male (sex_female=0) than in female (sex_female=1) patients.
Within KRAS G12C+ patients, the sotorasib benefit on pfs_months is attenuated by alk_fusion = 1 (sotorasib effect is smaller or absent when ALK is also positive).
Within KRAS G12C+ patients, the sotorasib benefit on pfs_months is attenuated by brca2_mutation = 1.

analyses.code: Subgroup means within kras_g12c==1; ols interaction term
analyses.result_summary: Sotorasib effect in KRAS+ males = +4.64 mo; in KRAS+ females = -0.012 mo. Interaction p < 1e-300. Strongly supported.

analyses.code: Subgroup means within kras_g12c==1 stratified by alk_fusion
analyses.result_summary: Sotorasib effect in KRAS+/ALK- = +2.67 mo; in KRAS+/ALK+ = -0.35 mo (n=87 treated). Interaction p = 4.1e-7.

analyses.code: Subgroup means within kras_g12c==1 stratified by brca2_mutation
analyses.result_summary: Sotorasib effect in KRAS+/BRCA2- = +2.63 mo; in KRAS+/BRCA2+ = +0.08 mo (n=66 treated). Interaction p = 6.1e-3.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The sotorasib treatment effect is fully concentrated in the joint subgroup defined by kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND alk_fusion=0 AND brca2_mutation=0; outside this subgroup the sotorasib effect is null.
The three-way interaction treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c × sex_female on pfs_months is significant and negative (sex_female cancels the sotorasib×kras_g12c benefit).

analyses.code: Define mask = (kras_g12c==1)&(sex_female==0)&(alk_fusion==0)&(brca2_mutation==0); compare sotorasib effect inside vs outside.
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=3266): sotorasib effect = +4.99 mo, p~0. Outside subgroup: sotorasib effect = -0.006 mo, p=0.76. Hypothesis strongly supported.
treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c = +4.66 (p~0); treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c:sex_female = -4.69 (p~0). Three-way interaction confirms women lose the entire KRAS-targeted sotorasib benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effects of pembrolizumab/olaparib/osimertinib do not interact with sex_female (no sex-modified main effect for these drugs).

Treatment effects of pembrolizumab/olaparib/osimertinib do not vary across any of the binary biomarkers (sex_female, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, egfr_mutation, kras_g12c, alk_fusion, stk11_mutation, brca2_mutation, tmb_high) — i.e., these treatments are inactive throughout the cohort.

analyses.code: For each tx in {pembro, olaparib, osimertinib}, ols('pfs_months ~ tx * sex_female')
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p-values: pembro×sex p=0.62; olaparib×sex p=0.80; osimertinib×sex p=0.61. None significant. Hypothesis supported.

analyses.code: Full grid of tx × binary-feature OLS interactions for the three drugs.
analyses.result_summary: Across 3 treatments × 9 binary features = 27 tests, only 1 nominally significant interaction (pembro × stage_iv, p=0.0068, not surviving multiple-testing for a coefficient of magnitude 0.108). All canonical biomarker matches (pembro×PD-L1-high, pembro×TMB-high, olaparib×BRCA2, osimertinib×EGFR) showed null interactions. Hypothesis supported: these three drugs appear inactive across the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous prognostic biomarkers (age_years, pdl1_tps, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl) do not modify the pembrolizumab, olaparib, or osimertinib treatment effects (no continuous-feature × treatment interaction).

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ tx * cont_feat') for each tx and continuous feat.
analyses.result_summary: Across 24 tests, only pembrolizumab × weight_loss_pct_6mo crossed nominal p<0.01 (coef +0.018, p=3.2e-4) — implies a tiny attenuation of pembro deficit at higher weight loss, magnitude clinically negligible (~0.2 mo across the IQR of weight loss). All other interactions p>0.05. Hypothesis broadly supported: no clinically meaningful continuous-feature modifiers for these three drugs.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive 2-way binary-feature subgroup search will reveal at least one subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 mo) and statistically significant (p<1e-3) treatment_pembrolizumab effect.
An exhaustive 2-way binary-feature subgroup search will reveal at least one subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 mo) and statistically significant (p<1e-3) treatment_olaparib effect.
An exhaustive 2-way binary-feature subgroup search will reveal at least one subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 mo) and statistically significant (p<1e-3) treatment_osimertinib effect.

analyses.code: Iterate over all (f1,v1)×(f2,v2) binary subgroup pairs with n>=200 and >=50 in each tx arm; record subgroups with |eff|>0.3 and p<1e-3 for treatment_pembrolizumab.
analyses.result_summary: Only one subgroup met the bar: kras_g12c=1 & stage_iv=0 (n=2265): pembro effect = -0.46 mo, p=1.5e-4. Effect is NEGATIVE (pembro associated with worse PFS in this subgroup), and the magnitude is small. No subgroup with positive pembro benefit found. Hypothesis refuted.
No 2-way binary subgroup met |eff|>0.3 and p<1e-3 thresholds. Hypothesis refuted: olaparib appears inactive even in fine-grained subgroups.
No 2-way binary subgroup met |eff|>0.3 and p<1e-3 thresholds. Hypothesis refuted: osimertinib appears inactive even in fine-grained subgroups, including its canonical EGFR-mutant target population.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive 2-way binary-feature subgroup search for treatment_sotorasib will identify the strongest sotorasib-benefit subgroup as kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 (KRAS G12C+ males), with effect > +4 mo.

analyses.result_summary: Top subgroup: kras_g12c=1 & sex_female=0 (n=3508): sotorasib effect = +4.64 mo, p~0. Next-best two-way subgroups all conditioned on kras_g12c=1 with effects ~+2.5–2.7 mo (e.g., kras_g12c=1 & alk_fusion=0 = +2.67; kras_g12c=1 & brca2_mutation=0 = +2.63). Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS for pfs_months including all features yields independent prognostic effects: ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, histology=squamous (negative); age_years, albumin_g_dl, former/never smoking (positive); with kras_g12c showing a positive coefficient and alk_fusion close to null after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: R-squared=0.894. Independent coefs: age=+0.168/yr, sex_female=-0.20, smoking former=+0.55, smoking never=+0.55, squamous=-0.72, ecog_ps=-1.10/unit, stage_iv=-1.42, has_brain_mets=-0.91, kras_g12c=+0.89, alk_fusion=-0.029 (ns), stk11=-0.019, brca2=-0.11, tmb_high=-0.005 (ns), albumin=+0.46 per g/dL, ldh=-4e-4 per U/L, weight_loss=-0.073 per pct. All other lab markers (crp, nlr, hgb, etc.) ns. Treatment main effects: pembro -0.006 (ns), sotorasib +0.32 (significant, driven by KRAS+ subgroup), olaparib +0.002 (ns), osimertinib +0.001 (ns). Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When the sotorasib three-way interaction terms (sotorasib×kras_g12c, sotorasib×kras_g12c×sex_female, sotorasib×kras_g12c×alk_fusion, sotorasib×kras_g12c×brca2_mutation) are included in a multivariable model, the sotorasib×kras_g12c benefit is large (>+4 mo) and the female/alk/brca2 sub-interactions are large negative (each >|2 mo|), confirming the multi-feature subgroup definition under joint adjustment.

analyses.code: ols with full feature set + sotorasib×kras_g12c, sotorasib×kras_g12c×sex_female, sotorasib×kras_g12c×alk_fusion, sotorasib×kras_g12c×brca2_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: treatment_sotorasib main = +0.001 (ns); sotorasib:kras_g12c = +4.77, p~0; sotorasib:kras_g12c:sex_female = -4.59, p~0; sotorasib:kras_g12c:alk_fusion = -2.41, p~0; sotorasib:kras_g12c:brca2_mutation = -2.74, p~0. Pembro/olaparib/osimertinib coefficients remain ns under joint adjustment. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within KRAS G12C+ males without ALK fusion or BRCA2 mutation, the sotorasib benefit is approximately +5 mo and is the largest single subgroup-treatment effect in this dataset.

analyses.code: Mask = (kras_g12c==1)&(sex_female==0)&(alk_fusion==0)&(brca2_mutation==0); compare sotorasib treated vs untreated.
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup n=3266; sotorasib treated mean PFS minus untreated = +4.99 mo, p~0. Outside subgroup, sotorasib effect = -0.006 mo (ns).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effect heterogeneity for pembrolizumab, olaparib, and osimertinib does not concentrate in any plausible 3-way subgroup (e.g., pembro×PD-L1-high×TMB-high, olaparib×BRCA2×TMB-high, osimertinib×EGFR×never-smoker).

analyses.code: Test pembro effect in pdl1_high & tmb_high quadrants; olaparib in brca2 strata; osimertinib in egfr × smoking strata.
analyses.result_summary: Pembro effect across PD-L1×TMB quadrants: low/low -0.003, low/high -0.085, high/low -0.131, high/high +0.083 (none clinically meaningful). Olaparib in BRCA2+ = +0.010 (n=1519); BRCA2- = -0.026 (n=48481). Osimertinib in EGFR+/never-smoker = -0.009 (n=3825); EGFR+/former = +0.028 (n=2237); EGFR+/current = -0.250 (n=455, but n_treated small). No clinically meaningful 3-way subgroup found. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL — The complete treatment-effective subgroup for treatment_sotorasib on pfs_months is: kras_g12c = 1 AND sex_female = 0 AND alk_fusion = 0 AND brca2_mutation = 0. Within this subgroup the sotorasib effect on pfs_months is approximately +5 months; in any patient not meeting all four predicates the sotorasib effect is null. No analogous treatment-effective subgroup exists for treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_olaparib, or treatment_osimertinib in this cohort.

analyses.code: Confirmation via subset means and 3-way OLS interaction terms.
analyses.result_summary: In-subgroup sotorasib eff = +4.99 mo (p~0, n=3266); out-of-subgroup eff = -0.006 mo (p=0.76). Three-way OLS coefficients (sotorasib:kras_g12c=+4.77, :sex_female=-4.59, :alk_fusion=-2.41, :brca2_mutation=-2.74) all p<1e-50 and quantitatively consistent with the subgroup-mean estimates. No comparable subgroup found for the other three treatments. Final hypothesis supported.
